front: What is a structure in C?
back: A structure is a user-defined data type that allows grouping of variables of different data types under a single name. It is defined using the `struct` keyword.

front: What is a union in C?
back: A union is a special data type that allows storing different data types in the same memory location. Only one member can contain a value at any given time.

front: What is the key difference between a structure and a union?
back: In a structure, each member has its own memory location, so the total size is the sum of all members. In a union, all members share the same memory location, so the total size is that of the largest member.

front: How do you access members of a structure or union variable?
back: Members are accessed using the dot operator (`.`) for a normal variable, or the arrow operator (`->`) for a pointer to a variable.

front: Why would you use a union instead of a structure?
back: Unions are used for efficient memory usage when you need to store different types of data but only one value at a time, such as in implementing variant data types or for interpreting the same data in multiple ways.
